description: The underground archaeological site on Beijing Road showcases the historical evolution of Guangzhou's thousand-year-old commercial district.
The most magnificent twin-spired Gothic stone Catholic church in China, known as the 'Oriental Notre-Dame de Paris.'
The Zhenhai Tower located within Yuexiu Park displays over 2,000 years of Guangzhou's urban development history.

attraction_name: Chen Clan Ancestral Hall (Guangdong Folk Art Museum)
description: A representative work of Lingnan architectural art from the Qing Dynasty, incorporating wood carving, brick carving, and ceramic sculpture.
A large integrated shopping and entertainment center featuring multiple restaurants, cinemas, and entertainment facilities.
A treasure of Lingnan architectural art from the Qing Dynasty, home to the Guangdong Folk Art Museum.
Guangzhou's most distinctive arcade-style commercial street, a hub for traditional Cantonese cuisine.
A large urban square on Guangzhou's new city axis, serving as the core landscape area of the CBD.
Ancient waterway scenery, showcasing Lingnan water town charm where traditional architecture harmoniously coexists with the modern city.
A typical Xiguan mansion-style building that exhibits traditional Xiguan folk culture and lifestyle.
